{{Quote|I have nothing but respect for the entire Single-Purpose series, Master Luke. Truly the core of droid operations. But they are so ''limited''.|[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]|<ref name="Children of the Jedi" />}}
'''SP Eighty cleaner droids''', also called '''SP-80 cleaners''', were a model of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] from the [[Single-Purpose series]] that were programmed with the single purpose of cleaning. When presented with something they considered a mess, they would attempt to clean it even if their limitations rendered them unable to do so. Though limited, the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] considered SP-80s and others of their series the core of droid operations.

SP-80 cleaners were blocky droids with [[Color/Legends|bronze]] colored plating, short legs, a single cleaner arm and a vacuum absorption pad, and a rotating upper body that was roughly cube-shaped and equipped with sensor squares that glowed blue when active. They were capable of identifying dirt and clutter visually, but also had a powerful ability to detect odors—said to be sensitive enough to scent a light stain measuring a mere fraction of a square centimeter at a range of over one hundred meters. A flip-open coverplate in the side concealed a comm cable input that allowed them to be easily reprogrammed.<ref name="Children of the Jedi" />

SP-80 cleaners were among the wide variety of droid models stationed aboard the ''[[Eye of Palpatine]]'' when [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and C-3PO investigated the massive [[Dreadnaught/Legends|Dreadnaught]]<ref name="Children of the Jedi" /> in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]].<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref>
